The Intern (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $75,764,672Details
International Box Office $121,351,038
Worldwide Box Office $197,115,710
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $10,163,465
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,239,489
Total $14,402,954
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Ben Whittaker, is a 70-year-old widower who has discovered that retirement isn’t all it’s cracked up to be. Seizing an opportunity to get back in the game, he becomes a senior intern at an online fashion site, founded and run by Jules Ostin.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,728,313 (23.4% of total gross)
Legs:4.27 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:38.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,305 opening theaters/3,320 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $107,483,718

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 25th, 2015 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: September 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Germany)
September 24th, 2015 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
September 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Switzerland)
September 24th, 2015 (Wide) (South Korea)
September 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Hungary)
Video Release: December 22nd, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some suggestive content and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2368, 4/8/2015)
Running Time: 121 minutes
Plot point: Autumn Years, Entrepreneur, Fashion, Internet, Out of Retirement
Social setting: Retirement
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Waverly Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Ben Whittaker, is a 70-year-old widower who has discovered that retirement isn’t all it’s cracked up to be. Seizing an opportunity to get back in the game, he becomes a senior intern at an online fashion site, founded and run by Jules Ostin.

Opening Weekend:$17,728,313 (23.4% of total gross)

Domestic Share:38.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:3,305 opening theaters/3,320 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $107,483,718
